reasoning: Based on economic data up to early 2024, the US experienced robust growth in 2023 (excluding a recession that year) and continued to show resilience in early 2024. A 'soft landing' consensus emerged among economists, significantly reducing the perceived risk of a 2024 recession compared to prior expectations. While it is theoretically possible that a recession began in late 2024 and is declared by the NBER by the end of 2025, the baseline probability for a recession starting in any given year is roughly 15-20%, and macro indicators suggested lower-than-average risk for the balance of 2024. Therefore, I estimate a 12% probability that a 2023/2024 recession occurred and was declared by the NBER by late 2025.
